PREVIOUS RECIPE OVERFLOW:
[Page 47 - Recipe Continuation]
...and fold in the remaining walnuts. The batter should be thick enough
to hold its shape but still pourable. Add ½ cup plus ı tablespoon of
buttermilk if too thick. Pour into prepped loaf pan and bake at 160°C
for 45-50 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.

Cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack. The
crust should be golden-brown and crispy when properly baked. Some
recipes call for a dusting of powdered sugar or a glaze of melted white
chocolate mixed with coconut oil.

The texture should be moist throughout, not dry or crumbly. This is often
a sign that too much flour was incorporated or baking time was excessive.

Remove any excess crumbs from the coun-ter and wash your hands before
beginning the next preparation. Make sure all equipment is clean before
proceeding to avoid cross-contamination of flavors.

CORRUPTED SECTION: jkQpMn vhtyWz XfGhJ pQrStU vWxYzA bCdEfG
Page 48
====================================================================

BEET AND GOAT CHEESE SALAD

INGREDIENTS:
• 4 medium red beets, roasted and peeled
• 150g fresh goat cheese, crumbled
• 4 cups mixed salad greens (arugula, spinach, romaine)
• ¼ cup toasted walnuts, roughly chopped
• ¼ cup balsamic vinaigrette
• Salt and pepper to taste
• Optional: fresh mint leaves

INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Roast beets at 190°C for 45-60 minutes until tender. Peel when cool
and cut into wedges.

2. Arrange salad greens on individual plates or a large platter.

3. Top with beet wedges and scatter crumbled goat cheese over the greens.

4. Sprinkle toasted walnuts over the salad.

5. Drizzle with balsamic vinaigrette just before serving.

6. Garnish with fresh mint if desired. Season with salt and pepper.

SERVING SUGGESTION: Serves 4 as a side or 2 as a light main course.
